E

Labcyte Echo

Echo 550 В· Cherry Pick В· Dose Response В· Data Analysis В· v2.0 В· Jun 2026

Dark mode
Echo 550
Beckman Coulter (formerly Labcyte) В· Acoustic Droplet Ejection В· 2.5 nL resolution В· Tip-free, contact-free В· DMSO and aqueous liquid classes
Software Modules
Cherry Pick
Transfer specific volumes from any source well to any destination well using a pick list. Accepts CSV, TXT, or XML. Multiple source plates supported per run; controls can be distributed to all assay plates via a separate pick list. Standard mode for compound library dispensing.
Module page вҶ—
Dose Response Standard
Creates dose-response curves using Direct Dilution вҖ” transfers are calculated proportionally from stock, not by serial dilution, so error does not compound across points. Input: stock concentrations and desired final concentrations. Use the Gradient Planner tab to design concentration series before running.
Module page вҶ—
Plate Reformat
Transfers and reformats sample libraries between plate formats (96-, 384-, 1536-well). Supports library compression (interleaved or quadrant patterns), custom well-to-well mapping, and concentration normalisation вҖ” the software calculates per-well volumes to hit a target concentration in the destination.
Module page вҶ—
Combination Screen
Designs combinatorial assay layouts for synergy and antagonism screening. Supports anchor, synergistic, and grid paradigms. Layers multiple dose-response curves onto a single plate with replicate and wrapping support. Outputs transfer protocols and 2D/3D combination layouts.
Module page вҶ—
Array Maker
Designs custom spotting patterns for non-standard destinations: arrays, chips, MALDI targets, protein crystallography slides. The user graphically defines the destination pattern; the software generates the transfer protocol for formats not possible with standard plate-to-plate modules.
Module page вҶ—
Plate Audit
Acoustic QC tool. Measures fluid composition and volume in every source well without performing transfers. Tracks changes across timepoints (evaporation, DMSO hydration drift). Outputs per-well pass/fail status and replenishment pick lists. Run before automated processing to qualify plates.
Module page вҶ—

Cat: 001-14555 В· PP В· 15 вҖ“ 65 ВөL В· DMSO / AQ
384 Low Dead Volume (LDV)
Cat: 001-12782 В· COC В· 2.5 вҖ“ 12 ВөL В· DMSO

Tips & Tricks
Always survey before a run
The built-in survey measures fluid height acoustically in every well before dispensing. Low-volume or empty wells (<15 ВөL in 384PP) will fail silently without it. Run Plate Audit to pre-qualify plates before a campaign.
Keep DMSO % equal across all wells
Add a DMSO backfill step after compound dispensing to bring every destination well to the same final DMSO concentration. Unequal DMSO causes assay artefacts that look like dose-response signal.
Direct Dilution beats serial dilution
Dose Response module calculates each concentration point directly from stock вҖ” errors don't compound across the curve as they do with serial dilution. Use intermediate plates (Gradient Planner) only when the volume difference between top and bottom points exceeds the max transfer limit.
Seal plates between uses
DMSO is hygroscopic вҖ” it absorbs water vapour from air, shifting concentration and degrading acoustic performance. Seal source plates with foil immediately after use and store at вҖ“20 В°C. The DMSO liquid class compensates for moderate hydration but not for heavily diluted stocks.
Equilibrate plates to room temperature
Cold plates (straight from вҖ“20 В°C) cause condensation and alter acoustic coupling. Let source plates equilibrate to ambient for 30вҖ“60 min before running. Cold fluid also shifts the acoustic profile, increasing transfer variability.
384PP vs 384LDV вҖ” choose wisely
Use 384PP for standard DMSO compound libraries (15вҖ“65 ВөL, PP material). Use 384LDV for precious or small-volume samples (2.5вҖ“12 ВөL, COC material). Dead volume in PP is ~15 ВөL; in LDV it is ~2вҖ“4 ВөL вҖ” factor this in when calculating how much sample you need.
Save the Echo transfer CSV
The Echo outputs a transfer report CSV after every run вҖ” this is the input for the Data Analysis module here. It records actual transferred volumes (not nominal), survey data, and any failed transfers. Archive it alongside your reader data for full traceability.
Match barcodes exactly
The analysis pipeline links Echo transfer data to PHERAstar reader files by destination plate barcode. The barcode in the Echo CSV must match the filename of the reader file exactly (case-sensitive). Mismatches silently drop entire plates from the analysis.
Top concentration limits
Max DMSO budget sets a ceiling on how much compound you can dispense. At 0.1% DMSO in a 20 ВөL assay the max transfer is 20 nL вҖ” only one 2.5 nL drop at a time. Use a lower stock concentration or increase DMSO tolerance if the top-dose transfer rounds to zero.
Replace coupling fluid regularly
The acoustic coupling fluid (distilled water + algaecide, Cat: ECHO-AL04) transmits ultrasound between the transducer and the plate. Contamination or evaporation degrades transfer accuracy. Replace per the maintenance schedule in the user manual.

Echo Data Analysis вҖ” User Guide
4PL curve fitting for HiBiT degradation assays В· Echo liquid handler + PHERAstar plate reader

Input files

Echo CSV
Transfer file from the Echo liquid handler. Required columns: Sample ID, Destination Plate Barcode, Destination Well, Destination Concentration (in M). The barcode links each transfer well to the matching PHERAstar plate.
PHERAstar XLS / XLSX
One file per assay plate. Filename (without extension) must exactly match the plate barcode in the Echo CSV. Luminescence data is read from rows 16вҖ“32, columns 2вҖ“26 (standard 384-well HiBiT layout).
SMILES CSV (optional)
Two-column file with Sample ID and SMILES headers. Enables structure display and computed ADMET/PK properties in the Properties tab.
вҡ— Load test data
Loads a built-in set of example files (Echo CSV + 6 PHERAstar plates) so you can explore the app without uploading real data. Useful for testing or demos.

Parameters

Assay ID
Free-text label used to name output files: Results_ID_Group.pdf. Use a date or experiment code. Leave blank for a generic name.
Control wells range
DMSO-only wells used as 100% reference. Signals are normalised per plate to the mean of these wells. Accepted formats: B12-O12 (column range), B22:O23 (rectangular block), B12,C12 (comma-separated). Leave blank to use raw luminescence without normalisation.
Echo header rows
Metadata rows above the column header in the Echo CSV. Default: 8 for standard Echo Plate Reformat output.
Hook effect threshold (О”%)
A hook effect occurs when HiBiT signal rises at the highest concentrations. If the mean signal at the highest concentration exceeds the mean at 2 steps lower by more than this many percentage points, that point is flagged (orange Г—) and excluded from fitting. E.g. threshold=10: ref=90% вҶ’ hook if >100%. Default: 10 О”%. Right-click the canvas in the Curves tab to re-include hook concentrations for any compound.
Min RВІ for fit
Minimum acceptable RВІ for a 4PL curve. Compounds below this are reported but flagged in red. Default: 0.8.
Max replicate SD
Maximum SD (in % DMSO) across replicates at any concentration. Compounds exceeding this are flagged as noisy. Default: 20.

Output values

DC50 (nM)
Concentration achieving 50% of maximal degradation вҖ” derived from the EC50 of the 4PL fit, converted to nM.
Dmax (%)
Maximum degradation at plateau = 100% вҲ’ bottom asymptote of the 4PL fit. A Dmax of 80% means 20% of target remains at saturating compound.
LogIC50 (M)
LogвӮҒвӮҖ of the inflection point (EC50 in molar units) from the 4PL fit. Useful for comparing potency across orders of magnitude on the scatter plot X-axis.
Hill slope
Cooperativity / steepness of the sigmoidal curve. Values near 1 are typical; values >2 may indicate steep cooperativity or data artefacts.
RВІ
Goodness of fit for the 4PL curve. Values below the Min RВІ threshold are highlighted in red in the results table.
PDF reports
One PDF per experimental group, one compound per page. Each page shows mean Вұ SD across replicates, 4PL fit line, DC50 / Dmax / RВІ stats box, hook-excluded points (Г—), and any QC flags.
CSV summary
Consolidated table with all fitted parameters for every compound. Includes Flag and Flag_Reason columns for full QC traceability.

Scatter & Selectivity plots

Plot type
Three modes: Scatter (any X vs Y parameter), Box plot (distribution per group), and Selectivity (Protein A vs Protein B for the same compound вҖ” see below).
X / Y axis
Choose any calculated parameter for each axis. Default view: X = LogIC50 (M), Y = Dmax (%) вҖ” potency vs. efficacy.
X / Y min / max
Number inputs to set axis limits manually. Leave blank for automatic scaling. Clear a value to revert to auto.
рҹ”Қ Search
Type a partial compound ID. Matching points highlight in yellow; non-matching points fade. A live match counter is shown next to the input.
Names
Toggle compound ID labels on data points. Labels use a text outline (no background box) to minimise overlap with neighbouring points.
Group
Draw a convex hull around each experimental group, colour-coded per group, for visual clustering.
Dot size
Slider to adjust the point radius.
PNG
Export a high-resolution PNG (1200 DPI equivalent). All active overlays (labels, hulls, reference lines) are included in the export.

Box plot

Y axis (metric)
Select which calculated parameter to display: DC50 (nM), Dmax (%), LogIC50 (M), Hill slope, or RВІ. Each compound contributes one box per group.
X axis (grouping)
Currently fixed to Group вҖ” compounds are grouped by their experimental group label (protein / target). Future versions may support custom grouping variables.
Group filter
Show all groups or restrict to a single group for a cleaner view when many targets are loaded.
Flagged
Toggle to include or exclude compounds that failed QC (low RВІ, noisy replicates, hook-only fits).
Y min / max
Set manual axis limits for the box plot Y-axis. Leave blank for automatic scaling.
PNG
Exports the current box plot as a PNG image.

Selectivity plot

What it shows
Plots Protein A value vs Protein B value for each compound shared between both datasets. Points on the diagonal (y=x) have equal activity on both targets; points above are selective for the Y protein; points below are selective for X.
Protein X / Y
Select which two experimental groups to compare. Populated automatically from your loaded data.
Metric
Any fitted parameter: pDC50, DC50 nM (log scale), Dmax %, Hill slope, RВІ. For log-scale metrics, the fold selectivity is computed as 10^|yвҲ’x|.
Selectivity bands
Coloured diagonal bands at ВұN log units from the y=x line. Green = selective for Y protein, red = selective for X protein, grey = within threshold. Band threshold is configurable (10Г—, 100Г—, ~3Г—).
Only shared compounds
When checked, only compounds with data in both Protein X and Protein Y are shown. Uncheck to also display compounds present in only one group (plotted at the axis edge).

Scatter tools

в–Ў Select
Draw a rectangle on the scatter plot to select compounds. Copy their Sample IDs to clipboard with рҹ“Ӣ Copy IDs. Press Escape or click Select again to cancel.
в¬Ү CSV (filtered)
Exports the currently visible (filtered) scatter data as a CSV file including all fitted parameters.
Undo / Redo filters
Each time you change a filter (group, axis range, search), the state is saved. Use Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y to step back and forward through filter history.
Curve on hover
When checked, hovering over a scatter point shows a mini 4PL curve preview for that compound in a floating panel.

Curves tab

Single / Compare modes
Single mode shows one compound at a time with full stats. Compare mode overlays multiple selected compounds. Scroll-wheel in single mode navigates between compounds.
Right-click вҖ” exclude rep
Right-click any individual replicate point to exclude it. Excluded points appear as grey Г— and are excluded from the refit. Right-click a grey Г— to re-include it.
Right-click вҖ” exclude conc
Right-click a mean point (or any rep) and choose Exclude all at this concentration to remove all replicates at that dose. Right-click вҶ’ Re-include all excluded to restore everything.
Right-click вҖ” hook effect
For compounds with a hook effect (orange Г— at highest doses), right-click anywhere on the canvas and choose Re-include hook effect concentrations to refit with all data.
Flag stats (right-click)
When a compound is flagged, the Flag row in the stats bar shows a в–ё indicator. Right-click it to Resolve flag (mark as accepted).
Fix Y axis
Check Fix Y and enter bottom/top values to lock the Y-axis range across compounds, useful for comparing curves on the same scale.
Compare вҖ” sort
In Compare mode, sort the compound list by DCвӮ…вӮҖ вҶ‘вҶ“ or Dmax вҶ‘вҶ“ using the sort selector above the list.

Plate view

Signal intensity
Colours each well by its normalised luminescence (%) using a viridis colour scale. Controls appear as green-tinted wells.
Raw signal QC
New. Colours wells by their robust z-score relative to the plate median (MAD-based). Blue = unusually low signal (possible empty well / poor cell dispensing); red = unusually high (double dispensing or background artefact). Ideal for catching dispensing errors before interpreting results.
DC50 / Dmax (fitted)
Colours each well by the fitted DC50 or Dmax of the compound at that position. Only populated after curve fitting.

Properties tab

Sortable columns
Click any column header to sort by that property (ascending вҶ’ descending вҶ’ reset). An arrow indicator (в–Ів–ј) shows the active sort column and direction.
SMILES
The SMILES cell shows a truncated string. Hover to see the full SMILES in a tooltip. Click to copy the full SMILES string to the clipboard.
Ro5
Lipinski Rule of Five: flags compounds with MW вүҘ 500, logP вүҘ 5, HBA > 10, or HBD > 5. Shows number of violations.

Tips

в“ҳ icons
Hover over any i icon next to a parameter label for a quick explanation of what it controls and how to set it.
Keyboard shortcuts
In the Curves tab: вҶҗ / вҶ’ to navigate between compounds; scroll to zoom; drag to pan; double-click to reset zoom. Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Y to undo/redo scatter filters.
Progress indicator
A small bat рҹҰҮ appears in the bottom-right corner during analysis, flapping to show the app is running. The progress bar shows N / Total curves fitted В· X% in real time.
